text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, semis and cyclicals quietly led. SOXX ripped +1.59% with metals, materials, and high-beta software strength signaling continued AI-and-commodities risk appetite despite only +0.54% on SPY and +0.59% on QQQ. STYLE showed SMALL > LARGE by 0.38pp, and breadth in XLB +1.87% and XLF +1.37% pointed to cyclicals catching a bid while USO -2.64% and XLE only +0.75% flagged lingering energy/geopolitics overhang.

INDICES — VIX 17.7 ticked slightly above its 20-day 17.5, staying mid-range. 1d performance ranked: SOXX +1.59% > IJR +0.97% > RSP +0.91% > IWM +0.87% > SLV +0.77% > DIA +0.73% > IJH +0.72% > QQQ +0.59% > SPY +0.54% > GLD +0.00%. STYLE confirmed small-cap outperformance with small +0.97% vs large +0.59%. Most notable cross-asset move was USO -2.64% versus UNG +1.70%, with VIXY -4.59% underscoring equity risk-on.

VOLUME — SPY volume was near-line with an indices average 1.17x while QQQ printed 1.4x, signaling incremental attention to large-cap growth. Elevated volume in DIA 1.9x and IWM 1.5x alongside price gains shows accumulation into cyclicals and small caps, while SOXX at 0.9x suggests semis’ +1.59% was a lower-conviction extension.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLB Materials +1.87%, XLF Financials +1.37%, XLU Utilities +1.09%, XLRE Real Estate +0.98%, XLK Technology +0.87%, XLE Energy +0.75%, XLP Staples +0.65%, XLI Industrials +0.59%, XLY Discretionary +0.26%, XLV Healthcare -0.18%, XLC Comms -0.42%. Top industries were SILJ +3.23% and GDX +2.97%, while bottom were DBC -1.04% and MSOS -4.36%. Money is rotating into materials, precious-metals miners, and financials while rotating out of cannabis and broad commodities. This is a broadening participation tape with “broad risk-on” character.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.73 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.05 with positive skews of +13.5% and +14.9% indicate notable put demand and downside hedging despite green tape. IWM PCR_vol 3.49 with skew +11.9% shows heavy small-cap protection demand, while VIX 17.7 sitting just above its 20-day 17.5 and MOVE 69.4 below its 20-day 75.5 points to equity caution against easing bond-vol stress.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -2.64% alongside XLE only +0.75% and OVX 54.1 below its 20-day 63.3 shows energy-equity resilience despite crude downside and still-elevated oil vol. SLV +0.77% with GLD flat and both 20d returns -18.83% and -7.37% respectively highlights a tentative, short-covering-style bounce in precious metals rather than a full macro shift.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed printed 69.4/100 Greed, with ADBE -6.76% [Software] and MCHI +2.71% [Countries] as standout single-name and country moves. 20d momentum extremes sit with SOXX +12.49% and XLC -3.82%, plus WGMI +3.21% [Industries] as a high-beta winner and MSOS -4.36% [Industries] lagging. ADBE’s 4.2x vol spike with -6.76% is clear distribution after raised FY2026 targets and buyback headlines reset expectations. Watch KLAC 6.4x vol, 0.00% for Fab-7 positioning tension, IYZ +1.27% on 3.4x vol for quiet telecom accumulation, and GREK 4.4x vol with +0.13% as a signal of growing peripheral-Europe risk appetite.
